prompt: A photorealistic, slightly surreal National Geographic photograph of a massive, dense flock of black starlings in a pale, overcast sky. A visual contradiction: every individual small cluster of birds is angled steeply downward, diving toward the ground. However, the macro-shape of the entire giant flock forms an unmistakable, sweeping formation soaring powerfully upward into the clouds. Uncanny, mind-bending geometry, highly detailed. No text, no arrows, no numbers.
visual_logic: Local groups of birds are clearly diving downward, but the global shape of the entire flock sweeps upward, capturing how local trends reverse when viewed as a combined whole.
